riskModel.reason: Broad dispatcher/accessor changes can affect gameplay, frontend, pause/menu, debug camera, recording/playback, and local multiplayer P1/P2 routing.
riskModel.allowedNextAction: Trace and observe only until a narrower hook or table row is proven.

riskModel.reason: DirectInput/deadzone changes are plausible but still have zero physical gamepad runtime proof on the current host.
riskModel.allowedNextAction: Fresh byte export and physical-device A/B proof before any player-facing deadzone claim.

riskModel.reason: These may change camera or gameplay balance rather than merely fixing control feel; source is hypothesis and retail runtime is authority.

riskModel.reason: These are copied-file, reversible, manifest-gated controls that already have read-back/materialization evidence.

recommendedNextRung.title: Attempt physical DirectInput/gamepad A/B proof before any deadzone patch row

claimBoundary: This map identifies bounded copied-profile original-binary control-feel candidates. It includes one file-backed static DirectInput deadzone byte-export candidate, but adds no visible patch row, performs no runtime proof, performs no Ghidra mutation, does not mutate the installed game or original BEA.exe, and does not prove improved control feel, physical gamepad behavior, wall-clock latency, true online multiplayer, active P3/P4 original-binary gameplay, rebuild parity, or no-noticeable-difference parity.
